Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are going to construct a Petri net for the insurance claim process described. Steps: 1. Create a Petri net object. 2. Create places for the start and end, and for each activity (or between activities) as needed. 3. Create transitions for each activity (CR, DV, FC, CA, AP, P, N, CL, RDS, II). 4. Connect places and transitions with arcs to represent the flow, including the loops for RDS and II. We note the following from the scenario: - The process starts with a claim (C) and then goes to Claim Registration (CR). - After CR, we have Document Verification (DV). - After DV, if documents are incomplete, we go to Re-Document Submission (RDS) and then back to DV. - After DV (if complete), we go to Fraud Check (FC). - After FC, if there are doubts, we go to In-depth Investigation (II) and then back to FC. This can happen multiple times. - After FC (if no doubts or after II), we go to Claim Assessment (CA).

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: After CA, we have Approval (AP) if the amount surpasses a certain threshold. Otherwise, we might skip AP? The scenario says must be approved by a senior manager if the amount surpasses a certain threshold. We are not given a condition for skipping, so we assume that AP is always required? But the scenario says if the amount surpasses a certain threshold. Lets assume that the decision is made in the CA activity and then we have two transitions: one for going to AP and one for going directly to Payment (P).

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We can model this by having a place after CA (p_after_CA). Then we have two transitions from p_after_CA: 1. One that leads to the AP activity (if above threshold). 2. One that leads directly to P (if below threshold). We can have two transitions from p_after_CA: one labeled AP and one labeled P. From AP, we go to P. We can model the payment activity as a transition that is enabled from two different places. Since the problem is about constructing the Petri net and not about the data conditions, we can assume that the condition is internal to the CA activity and then we have two transitions from the output place of CA.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: I will choose to model it with two different transitions for payment (one after AP and one direct) and both labeled P. This is because in the scenario, the payment activity is the same, but the context is different. Alternatively, we can have one transition for payment that is enabled from two different places by using a XOR-split and then merging the two places into one with a silent transition? But then we are adding silent transitions. Since the problem does not specify, I will avoid silent transitions and use two transitions labeled P.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: For the loops: after DV, either RDS back to DV, after FC either II back to FC. So we need two transitions for DV (from p_after_CR and from p_after_RDS both labeled DV), similarly for FC. Places: p_start, p_after_CR, p_after_DV, p_after_RDS, p_after_FC, p_after_II, p_after_CA, p_after_AP, p_paid, p_after_N, p_final. Transitions: t_CR from p_start to p_after_CR label CR, t_DV1 from p_after_CR to p_after_DV label DV, t_RDS from p_after_DV to p_after_RDS label RDS, t_DV2 from p_after_RDS to p_after_DV label DV, t_FC1 from p_after_DV to p_after_FC label FC, t_II from p_after_FC to p_after_II label II, t_FC2 from p_after_II to p_after_FC label FC, t_CA from p_after_FC to p_after_CA label CA, t_AP from p_after_CA to p_after_AP label AP, t_P_direct from p_after_CA to p_paid label P, t_P from p_after_AP to p_paid label P, t_N from p_paid to p_after_N label N, t_CL from p_after_N to p_final label CL.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: When a token is in p_after_DV, both t_RDS and t_FC1 are enabled. Similarly from p_after_FC, t_II and t_CA. In reality, choose based on condition, but in Petri net structure, non-deterministic choice. In process mining, conditions attached to transitions. We are only building the structure. Initial marking: token in p_start. Final: p_final.
